page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.i2.2xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 8 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, providing 8 vCPUs. The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. A key hardware feature is the inclusion of 1919 GB of bundled local SSD storage. Network performance is defined by a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps, and virtualization is managed via the KVM hypervisor.

Benchmark results indicate a clear tradeoff between storage capacity and raw processing power. The server's single-core and multi-core CPU performance, as measured by stress-ng and Geekbench, falls into the bottom 25% tier. Similarly, single-threaded gzip compression is poor, ranking in the bottom 10%. However, memory bandwidth for large blocks (both read and write) and Passmark CPU encryption tests achieve average performance. Redis SET operations also perform at an average level, handling 6.06 million operations per second.

Given the high density of local SSD storage and the 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance type offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that require significant local data caching or medium-sized database deployments rather than compute-intensive processing. The weak CPU physics and database operations scores suggest it is less suited for high-transaction relational databases or heavy computational tasks. Instead, the server is best aligned with workloads such as local caching, read-heavy database replicas, static web serving, or lightweight data processing where storage capacity and memory footprint are prioritized over CPU speed.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.i2.2xlarge is an x86_64 server configured with 8 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz, 64.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 1919 GB local SSD. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Performance benchmarks reveal a distinct tradeoff: while large-block memory bandwidth and Redis SET operations achieve average results, raw compute performance in Geekbench and stress-ng tests falls into the bottom 25% tier. This resource profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for storage-heavy and memory-dependent tasks rather than compute-bound applications. The server is best suited for workloads such as local caching, read-heavy database replicas, and lightweight data processing where local SSD capacity and a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio are critical.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.i2.2xlarge is an x86_64 server with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 1919 GB of local SSD storage, offering a storage-focused profile with moderate compute performance.

tagline: An x86_64 server featuring 1919 GB of bundled local SSD storage and 64 GB of RAM for storage-centric workloads.

64.0 GB of system memory providing an 8.0 GB per core ratio
1919 GB of bundled local SSD storage for high-capacity local caching
2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on a KVM hypervisor
Best suited for read-heavy database replicas and local storage workloads
